# Vendored: ruvector_temporal_tensor_wasm — (NOT vendored; pure-JS fallback used)

This directory is deliberately empty apart from this note. P1.C ("Temporal
sensor trajectory embedding") named the upstream `ruvector-temporal-tensor-wasm`
crate as its WASM dependency, but two things came up when we tried to vendor it
via `scripts/vendor-ruvector.sh`:

1. **No `wasm-bindgen` bindings.** The crate exports a raw `extern "C"` FFI
   (see `ruvector/crates/ruvector-temporal-tensor/src/ffi.rs`:
   `ttc_create`, `ttc_push_frame`, `ttc_flush`, …). `wasm-pack build` needs
   `#[wasm_bindgen]` annotations to generate the JS glue this repo relies on
   for its no-build, browser-only delivery. Without glue we'd have to
   hand-write `WebAssembly.instantiate` + raw-pointer memory plumbing on the
   JS side, which defeats the point of the vendored-artefact pattern.

2. **Upstream workspace doesn't include the wasm crate.** Even if we added
   wasm-bindgen bindings, `ruvector-temporal-tensor-wasm` isn't listed in
   `workspace.members` of the upstream `Cargo.toml`. Adding it upstream is
   out of scope for a car-learning demo PR.

**What we shipped instead.** `AI-Car-Racer/dynamicsEmbedder.js` — a pure-JS
implementation that records a per-frame `[ray0..ray4, speed, steering, throttle]`
trajectory for the current best car, then reduces it to a 64-dim vector via
per-channel statistical features (mean, std, min, max, mean|Δ|, max|Δ|, p25,
p75) and L2-normalises. The temporal-tensor crate's job was *compression* of
per-frame tensors at scale; for a browser-local archive of a few hundred
brains, the raw statistical projection is equivalent behaviour at effectively
zero CPU cost and no wasm round-trip.

If you later want to pick this up — e.g. to persist the full per-frame
trajectory for replay — the two outstanding tasks are:

- Add `#[wasm_bindgen]` wrappers around the `ttc_*` FFI in the upstream crate
  (or a thin shim crate inside `ruvector-temporal-tensor-wasm`).
- Add `crates/ruvector-temporal-tensor-wasm` to `ruvector/Cargo.toml`'s
  `workspace.members`.

Then `scripts/vendor-ruvector.sh ruvector/crates/ruvector-temporal-tensor-wasm`
will produce a real vendored artefact and this file can be regenerated by the
script.
